descript.abstract: The FIRESTAT (Fire Statistics System) Fire Occurrence point layer represents ignition points, or points of origin, from which individual wildland fires started on National Forest System lands. The source is the FIRESTAT database, which contains records of fire occurrence, related fire behavior conditions, and the suppression actions taken by management taken from the Individual Wildland Fire Report. This publicly available dataset is updated annually for all years previous to January 1 on or after February 16th.
descript.purpose: The FIRESTAT database contains current year and historical (dating back to 1987) Forest Service fire occurrence data and is used in analysis and reporting. The FIRESTAT point feature class is useful in making land and resource management decisions, in fire planning, and in quality control of the tabular database. It can be used to produce spatially enabled business intelligence reports.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

dataqual.logic: The dataset adheres to the data standards outlined in the Forest Service Fire Statistics System (FIRESTAT) User Guide For The Individual Wildland Fire Report, FS-5100-29, pp. 1-87, 5/16/2016.
dataqual.complete: Data is complete as of publication.

procstep.procdesc: An Individual Wildland Fire Report (FS-5100-29) is prepared for each unplanned ignition that burns or threatens to burn on National Forest System lands or on lands for which the Forest Service has protection or responsibility. The origin (latitude/longitude) is input to the form. These coordinates are then converted to a point feature class and automatically updated using an Extract/Transform/Load (ETL) procedure.

enttyp.enttypd: Feature class: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Assigned name of the Fire.
Local fire number assigned when fire is discovered. For local use only.
Text description of the fire location, for local use only.
Full township number 001 to 199. Includes N and S direction.
Full range number 001 to 199. Includes E and W direction.
The section number.
The smallest subdivision to the nearest quarter-section (160 acres) and quarter-quarter (40 acres). Example: SWNE.
Principal meridian code from PRINCIPAL_MERIDIANS table in FIRESTAT.
Concatenation of Region and Forest codes.
Name of forest
Number that identifies the Ranger District.

Fire Number. Part of the unique key in FIRESTAT. Description and action to be taken: Enter the Supervisor's Office (SO) fire number, a number from 001 to 999 (must enter leading zeroes). The SO fire number should be the next available consecutive number. Supervisor's Office numbers should be consecutive, without gaps, unless there is a significant local need to do otherwise. Gaps in the numbering sequence often cause future questions over whether or not the fire record for a Forest is complete. This information, combined with the year of discovery, give each fire report a unique identity. Note: The fire number is not generated by the system, which greatly increases the likelihood of gaps in consecutive numbering. If a duplicate number is entered, a system error message appears.
Agency administrative unit number.
Agency administrative unit name.
The agency or category of agency that is legally responsible for providing primary protection for the land at the point and time of origin.
Protection Unit ID at fire origin.
Protection Unit name at fire origin.
The land ownership type at the point of origin.
Ownership Unit ID at fire origin.
Ownership Unit name at fire origin.
Historical field (not currently used).
State Postal code
State or other jurisdiction at the point of origin.

attrdomv.udom: Two-letter state code and full name

attr.attrdef: County or Equivalent Government
The county at the point of origin.
Combined County and State Code
Fire Management Zone code.
Plan of Operation (POO) Latitude
Plan of Operation (POO) Longitude
Point of origin latitude degrees.
Point of origin latitude minutes
Point of origin latitude seconds
Point of origin longitude degrees
Point of origin longitude minutes
Point of origin longitude seconds
Discovery Calendar Year. DISCOVER_YEAR is part of unique key in FIRESTAT.
Date and time when the fire started.
Date and time when the fire was discovered.
Initial Response Date/Time.
Fire detector method code. Selected from FIRE_DETECTORS_CODE table in FIRESTAT.
The name of the lookout or person who discovered the fire.
Statistical cause of the fire. Old values from before web version of FIRESTAT.
Initial strategy selected from INITIAL_SUPP_STRATEGY table in FIRESTAT, which has changed over time.
Date and time when the initial suppression action was achieved.
Land or fire management plan objectives, which may or may not involve taking on-the-ground action.

The objective for reporting fires managed in a complex or that burn together, i.e. merge, is to simplify the workload for users, while representing the acres burned, by providing several possible scenarios for report completion.
Name of the fire complex.
Suppression strategy met. Not used after 1992.
Date and time when the fire was declared out. Must be later (greater) than STRATEGY_MET.
FS acres the total acres of Forest Service land burned on all Forests. To the nearest hundredth (0.00) acre.
Number of acres of non-National Forest System lands protected by the Forest Service within the fire perimeter. To the nearest hundredth (0.00) acre.
Number of acres of non-National Forest System lands outside of Forest Service protection within the fire perimeter. To the nearest hundredth (0.00) acre.
Total acres burned (integer).
The Fire Class Size field is auto-populated based on the number of acres in the Total Acres Burned field.

edom.edomvds: Forest Service Fire Statistics System (FIRESTAT) User Guide For the Individual Wildland Fire Report, FS-5100-29, 2015.
Forest Service Fire Statistics System (FIRESTAT) User Guide For the Individual Wildland Fire Report, FS-5100-29, 2015.
Forest Service Fire Statistics System (FIRESTAT) User Guide For the Individual Wildland Fire Report, FS-5100-29, 2015.
Forest Service Fire Statistics System (FIRESTAT) User Guide For the Individual Wildland Fire Report, FS-5100-29, 2015.
Forest Service Fire Statistics System (FIRESTAT) User Guide For the Individual Wildland Fire Report, FS-5100-29, 2015.
Forest Service Fire Statistics System (FIRESTAT) User Guide For the Individual Wildland Fire Report, FS-5100-29, 2015.
Forest Service Fire Statistics System (FIRESTAT) User Guide For the Individual Wildland Fire Report, FS-5100-29, 2015.

attr.attrdef: Indicator for prescribed fire.
Number of acres burned under prescription using the rules that govern wildland fire for resource benefit. To the nearest hundredth (0.00) acre.
Indicates whether the fire burned acres within the WUI as based on the accepted regional/unit definition.
Acres Burned in Wildland Urban Interface (WUI). Number of acres burned, to the nearest one hundredth.
Fire intensity code for the predominant or typical sustained average flame length observed at the head of the fire during the initial attack. Selected from FIRE_INTENSITY_LEVELS table in FIRESTAT.

attrdomv.udom: Code and description of flame length.

attr.attrdef: Six-digit weather station.

attrdomv.udom: ID selected from WEATHER_STATION table in FIRESTAT.

attr.attrdef: Station type code.
Text field with station named (seldom used).
Wind speed (field not used after 1985).
NFDRS (National Fire Danger Rating System) Fuel model. Selected from FIRE_FUELS_MODELS table in FIRESTAT.
A secondary Fuel Model code.
Regional vegetation cover type where the fire burned during the initial attack.
Slope expressed as a percent (rise over distance) between 0 and 150.
The direction that the landscape predominantly faces at a defined position, such as the center point of the occurrence.
Elevation in feet at the head of the fire at initial action stage. Whole number with no decimal values.
Local timezone where the fire occurred.
Timestamp of when the record was entered in FIRESTAT.
Date the report was submitted.
Date the report was approved.
Date record created
Date Report Last Modified
Feature geometry.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
